diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, and other meaty foods. Has been reported to accept high-quality marine flake and pellet foods readily in captivity.

summary: The Ruby Longfin Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus rubeus) is a small, colorful reef fish from the central Indian Ocean, recognized by the male's vivid red-orange body and distinctively elongated pelvic fins. It is generally considered peaceful and well suited to community reef aquariums.
careNotes: The Ruby Longfin Fairy Wrasse takes its name from the male's vivid red-to-red-orange body and the distinctively elongated pelvic fins that set it apart from other fairy wrasses. Color intensity and patterning can vary among individual specimens depending on origin and mood, and the species is sometimes sold under the alternate trade name Blue and Red Fairy Wrasse. Like all fairy wrasses in the genus Cirrhilabrus, this species is a protogynous hermaphrodite — meaning individuals begin life as females, and dominant fish later transition to male. Males tend to display more intense coloration than females. Multiple individuals generally coexist well when introduced to the aquarium at the same time. A tightly fitting lid is strongly recommended, as fairy wrasses are known to jump.
